authors: Kayo Mimizuka Katlyn Glover Inga K. Trauthig Samuel C. Woolley a School of Journalism and Media
The University of Texas at Austin
USAc Jack D. Gordon Institute for Public Policy
raw_title: “I Don’t Even Watch News (Voluntarily) on WeChat” – Socially-Compelled Exposure to Misinformation Among U.S. Diaspora Communities
title_en: “I Don’t Even Watch News (Voluntarily) on WeChat” – Socially-Compelled Exposure to Misinformation Among U.S. Diaspora Communities
title_zh: “我甚至（自愿地）不在微信上看新闻”——美国侨民社区中社会性被迫接触错误信息
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了美国侨民社区成员如何通过微信等社交媒体平台，非自愿地接触到错误信息。研究发现，即使人们不想看新闻，也可能因为社交压力或群体互动而被迫看到虚假内容。
plain_summary_en: This study examines how members of U.S. diaspora communities are involuntarily exposed to misinformation through social media platforms like WeChat. The research finds that even when people don't want to consume news, they may be forced to see false information due to social pressure or group interactions.
research_direction_zh: 研究数字时代中，社交媒体如何通过社会互动机制影响特定群体（如移民社区）的信息接触模式，特别是非自愿接触错误信息的现象及其社会心理影响。
research_direction_en: Investigating how social media influences information exposure patterns among specific groups (such as immigrant communities) in the digital age, particularly the phenomenon of involuntary exposure to misinformation and its socio-psychological impacts through social interaction mechanisms.
